items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения» and keeps the clipped parallelism «У Шпака — …, у посла — …» that drives the comedic callback. No further evaluation needed per instructions.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It keeps the repetitive, banal Soviet-politeness rhythm that creates the era-clash humor and conveys Bunsha’s drunken, socially automatic self-introduction.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. The imperative ‘I demand’ correctly captures the sudden authoritarian royal persona and preserves the punchy comedic force.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It maintains the elevated/archaic melancholy tone and the comic mismatch with the modern setting; ‘old woman’ keeps the intended insult/era-gap.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It correctly makes ‘demons’ the agent and preserves the panicked, superstitious misreading of technology that is central to the joke.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It keeps the mock-biblical proclamation tone and the religious framing that heightens the Soviet-era irony.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It conveys the MC-like command to start dancing and keeps the mass-invitation energy that fits the scene’s comic release.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It captures Miloslavsky’s breezy self-satisfaction and opportunistic cheer without over-explaining, preserving the comic understatement.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It correctly avoids calling the eggplant item ‘caviar’ in English (which would miscue the joke) while preserving the build-up and punchline inversion.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It preserves the comic inventory cadence, the escalating counts, and the slightly incoherent add-on ‘domestic...’ that signals Shpak’s performative exaggeration.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved/reference translation. It keeps the calm, sing-song resignation and the shared-absurdity punch that turns a dark institutional reality into comedy.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
